items.title: Pickett takes mark of the year contender in Demon's stunning outing
items.summary: Melbourne eases to a 54-point over Richmond at the MCG, as the hapless Tigers remain the competition's only side without a win in 2026. Follow live.

items.title: Bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton flowers
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care at Modbury Hospital
items.summary: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

items.title: Massive reduction in road safety offences thanks to AI-assisted cameras, WA government says
items.summary: The cameras have handed out around 184,000 infringements since they started issuing fines in October last year, according to the WA government.

items.title: NSW star Kezie Apps says Women's State of Origin timing 'not ideal' after six-month lay-off for players
items.summary: Kezie Apps is one of a number of Origin stars who will play her first match in a matter of months when she represents the Blues, and she doesn't believe the lay-off is the best preparation.

items.title: Norway and Türkiye follow Australia with youth social media bans
items.summary: Several European nations seek to rein in children's use of social media after Australia introduced its ban last December.

items.title: Oyster growing areas closed again on the Yorke Peninsula due to toxic phytoplankton
items.summary: Oyster growing areas on the Yorke Peninsula are ordered to close due to the detection of a toxic phytoplankton for the second time this year.

items.title: Markets 'hanging on' to a possible Iran war resolution

items.title: India wants to redraw its electoral map for the first time in decades, but could this marginalise its southern states?
items.summary: India's government proposed a plan to get more women into parliament but it came with one major condition.

items.title: Injury-decimated Broncos score spectacular tries to heap more pain on Dogs
items.summary: Injury-hit Brisbane stands tall at a wet and wild Lang Park, scoring two remarkable tries in a 32-12 defeat of Canterbury-Bankstown.

items.title: 'Amazing' horse-led Anzac Day march tradition continues over two decades

items.title: Berri Barmera councillor convicted of drink driving able to keep licence until after Anzac Day
items.summary: Berri Barmera Councillor Ian Schlein will hand in his licence several days after a regional court convicted him of drink driving, to allow him to attend an Anzac Day parade.

items.title: Why the gas tax momentum is building as Australia looks to properly cash in on its abundant natural resources
items.summary: Gas giants are breathing a sigh of relief after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ruled out a 25 per cent tax on exports in next month's budget. But for how long will the federal government resist something that one leading expert says is in our "national interest"?

items.title: Gas giant Woodside says additional tax on gas exports would kill projects at Senate inquiry
items.summary: Oil and gas giant Woodside denies its business model is "weak" while arguing that paying a proposed flat tax on gas exports would kill the company's projects.

items.title: AFL 'specifically rejects' appeal board chair's comments regarding homophobic slurs after Lance Collard verdict
items.summary: Both the AFL and the AFL Players' Association hit back at suggestions from the appeals board that slurs are commonplace among players in the sport.

items.title: Defamation trial shown messages between Rebel Wilson and film producer
items.summary: A Sydney court is shown a text message exchange between Hollywood star Rebel Wilson and a movie producer over a bathroom incident involving a young actor.

items.title: Conductor, composer and pianist Michael Tilson Thomas, known for work with San Francisco Symphony, dies aged 81
items.summary: The celebrated conductor led the San Francisco Symphony for 25 years, and co-founded the New World Symphony.

items.title: Camper describes moment 'brazen' dingo attacked a child in outback WA
items.summary: Kristen Bennetts says a dingo came dangerously close to her family during a night camping at Karijini National Park. Then, she heard a "horrendous scream".

items.title: Kyle Sandilands returns to court over termination of ARN Media contract
items.summary: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson have separately launched legal proceedings following the termination of their $100 million contracts.

items.title: Failing on social mobility 'is inefficiency with a human face'
items.summary: Is reducing inequality and increasing social mobility one of Australia's best paths to improving our lacklustre economic productivity performance?

items.title: Perth woman warns about pedicure safety after severe infection almost costs her a toe
items.summary: Kyla Willcox says she almost lost her toe from a potentially fatal infection after having a pedicure at a Perth nail salon.

items.title: Horse trainer wants Australia to remember light horsemen, their 'amazing' companions this Anzac Day
items.summary: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

items.title: Newmont's Cadia gold mine aims to fix earthquake damage in five weeks
items.summary: Underground operations at Newmont's Cadia gold mine are set to resume in five week's time, after a record earthquake that shook Central West NSW.

items.title: 100-year-old bugler prepares for Anzac Day ceremonies in regional Victoria town
items.summary: A Boort centenarian prepares to sound his bugle this Anzac Day at local schools and the RSL.

items.title: Dozen more Aboriginal burial sites detected amid construction on Rottnest Island
items.summary: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

items.title: Man who helped fellow runner across Boston Marathon finish line says it was natural to help
items.summary: Aaron Beggs, who helped fellow runner Ajay Haridasse across the Boston Marathon finish line, says "the natural instinct was just to go and pick him up".

items.title: Tobacco ban in the UK could create a smoke-free generation
items.summary: Anyone born after 2008 will be banned from buying cigarettes or vapes for life in the UK when a new bill becomes law.

items.title: Yamatji Vietnam veteran Graham Taylor remains brothers in arms with platoon mates from 1971
items.summary: Yamatji man Graham Taylor was just 19 when he shipped off to Vietnam. More than 50 years on, the brotherhood forged in the jungle of Vietnam remains solid.

items.title: Pentagon awards $276m submarine contract in first for AUKUS pact
items.summary: The US Department of Defense announces the first major submarine contract to deliver on the AUKUS security deal.

items.title: US soldier charged with making $560,000 on Nicolás Maduro removal with Polymarket bets
items.summary: In the weeks leading up to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro's January 3 capture, Gannon Ken Van Dyke used sensitive classified information to make wagers on prediction market Polymarket.

items.title: Resilient Queensland bush community keeps Eidsvold Cattle Drive alive
items.summary: A dedicated band of volunteers is keeping a cattle drive tradition alive to raise money for vital health services.

items.title: Italy dismisses Trump official's 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at football World Cup
items.summary: Italian officials say they are not interested in replacing Iran at the FIFA World Cup, after the idea was raised by a Trump ally.

items.title: Sick kangaroos isolated by urban growth on NSW Mid North Coast
items.summary: Kangaroos lying on a NSW mid north coast headland may be a "selfie" dream for tourists, but some are becoming weak from malnutrition.

items.title: Whale rescuers prepare for increased entanglements during annual migration
items.summary: As thousands of whales begin their annual northern migration up the east coast of Australia, teams of specialist rescuers are preparing to help them along the way.

items.title: Mapping the most critical shipping lanes in the world
items.summary: Oil prices have spiked since the Strait of Hormuz was closed. With about 90 per cent of all traded goods shipped by sea, what are the other key shipping lanes in the world?

items.title: Two people arrested after $13m drug seizure on Hume Highway
items.summary: A man and woman in their 20s have appeared in court after allegedly being found with 33 kilograms of methylamphetamine.

items.title: The 10 best books out in April, featuring Patrick Radden Keefe and Caro Claire Burke
items.summary: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

items.title: Is it ever OK to use someone else's toothbrush?
items.summary: About half of us have shared a toothbrush — usually between spouses, or parents and children. We asked the experts if it's a good idea, even in an emergency.

items.title: Prince Harry urges Putin to 'stop this war' on visit to Ukraine
items.summary: Prince Harry makes a surprise visit to Kyiv, urging Russia's President Vladimir Putin to end the war and calling on US President Donald Trump to show leadership to help resolve the conflict in Ukraine.

items.title: Victoria Cross recipient Keith Payne calls for federal government to step up veteran support
items.summary: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers has called for a national rethink on the care of veterans and their families.

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street losses, oil prices spike again — as it happened
items.summary: Australia's ASX 200 has ended the week on a disappointing note, although the market clawed back most of its earlier losses to finish less than 0.1 per cent down for the day. Meanwhile, oil prices are rising again with Brent crude trading above $US105 a barrel. Look back on day's events with the ABC News markets blog.

items.title: Appeal board says homophobic and racist language commonplace in AFL due to 'highly competitive' nature of the game
items.summary: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

items.title: Alison didn't recognise the symptoms of her stroke. She wants to stop others making the same mistake
items.summary: When Alison Reidy, then 38, woke in terrible pain, she had no idea she was having a stroke. Now she's trying to improve stroke awareness in the Northern Territory.

items.title: Regional providers, families fear they will be hit hardest by cuts to NDIS
items.summary: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

items.title: Adam Walton earns big Madrid Open victory, Alexei Popyrin's 2026 season struggles continue
items.summary: Queenslander Adam Walton has enjoyed a fine win at the Madrid Open to arrest a miserable sequence of Aussie losses at the clay-court event.

items.title: Iran daily briefing: Israel, Lebanon peace talks, Trump's 'shoot and kill' order, and what else to know
items.summary: Talks in Washington are set against a backdrop of decades-long disputes and deep mistrust, but at least they're happening.

items.title: Fuel prices in Australia: how rising costs are forcing Australians to change their habits
items.summary: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

items.title: Adelaide Festival appoints new Writers' Week director in wake of crisis
items.summary: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

items.title: Tasmania Police sergeant used dispatch system in 'relentless' monitoring of wife in family violence matter
items.summary: Text messages read in court set out how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

items.title: When is the Anzac Day public holiday? Which states have Monday off?
items.summary: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

items.title: Wallaroos ready to face New Zealand in Anzac Day Test after wild weather on United States tour
items.summary: Having survived the threat of tornadoes during their US tour, the Wallaroos are enjoying a distraction-free build-up to their Anzac Day Test against New Zealand.

items.title: These fishermen in the South China Sea get paid for their time on water — not to fish
items.summary: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

items.title: Matildas goalkeeper Chloe Lincoln on finding her voice and commanding her idols
items.summary: The A-League Women finals kick off this weekend, with Brisbane to make its first appearance in five years.

items.title: Today in history, April 24: Jane Fonda's important place in fitness industry thanks to workout video
items.summary: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot
items.summary: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

items.title: Power price warning as proposed capital gains tax changes create 'uncertainty' for foreign investors
items.summary: The treasurer says changes to capital gains tax for foreign investors is a clarification, but experts say it could dampen investment.

items.title: Baby boomers beware: Labor is reading the room on generational inequity
items.summary: The Albanese government is reading the room when it comes to intergenerational equity ahead of the May 12 budget.

items.title: NDIS fraud crackdown will flip rules on their head for 'let it rip' provider market
items.summary: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will end the practice of handwritten invoices, providers without qualifications and a "let it rip" market of profiteering operators. But the sector warns changes must be navigated carefully.

items.title: NDIS cuts leave participants concerned about what lies ahead
items.summary: NDIS participants set up their lives around the scheme's rules. But now the rules are changing and they fear what lies ahead.

items.title: Four charts that paint a worrying picture for global fertiliser prices
items.summary: Farmers around the world are going to be paying more for one of their most important inputs, fertiliser. Here are four charts that explain why.

items.title: Viva Energy boss defends fuel price surge, says more refineries needed for secure supply
items.summary: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief executive, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

items.title: Four 'finfluencers' suspected of giving unlawful advice hit with warning notices amid regulator crackdown
items.summary: The warning notice to the so-called "finfluencers" relates to suspected provision of unlicensed financial advice, including promoting claims of guaranteed returns.

items.title: Hegseth 'not anxious for a deal', says US blockade on Iran 'going global' — as it happened
items.summary: Pete Hegseth said the US was "not anxious" for a deal with Iran — as it happened.
